div.note: Subject;- Morale Report.
To;- HQ.,
Land. Forces Adriatic,
C.M.F.
CONFIDENTIAL
Adv. HQ.,
Special Boat Service,
L.F.A. C.M.F.
Ref: ASBS/3/131
Sec. I. MORALE IN GENERAL.

The morale of the Unit for the period under review has been high, in spite of operational setbacks. As a result of the end of the war in Europe morale is expected to remain at a high level. During this period we have been able to have all three Ss in the field, although in Jugoslavia it was only possible to operate one Sqn at a time: when permitted by our Jugoslav allies to show their worth these Sqns acquitted themselves well. Morale of the last Sqn to go there was not aided by the seemingly unwarranted house arrest of this Sqn, including the C.O. of the Unit.
Under these circumstances it was difficult for those in authority to stimulate interest in the men to devote their efforts whole-heartedly to prosecuting the war successfully in that theatre.
Sec. II. FACTORS EFFECTING MORALE. hhh 1. Discipline.
Discipline is bound to be extremely high in a Special Service Unit: indeed, it is the "be-all and end-all" of this type of unit in the field. hhh 2. Confidence.
(a) In Army Leaders and the War. In this respect confidence has always been of a high order and this has shown itself to be fully justified.

(b) In Arms and Equipment. - As we have always been fortunate enough to have access to the best and latest types of arms and equipment, confidence in them is always high, knowing that better things will be produced if confidence wanes.
(c) In Efficiency. - Without confidence in one's own efficiency it is difficult to see how one could operate at all hhh 3. Mail.
Extremely good recently. hhh 4. Welfare and. Entertainments.
(a) Living Conditions. In our case living conditions are sound as we have taken over a camp which is well provided with necessaries. For the first time for two years the entire unit is together.
(b) Recreation and Amusements. The former now plays a most important part in keeping troops amused: the unit indulges in Soccer, Basketball, Swimming, Boxing etc. Amusements on the whole are good.
(c) General. The unit is well provided-for in Welfare and we are able to get a large amount of welfare stores. hhh 5. Education and ABCA.
Operational and training commitments have previously prevented much being done in this way, but now it is hoped to organise something in this connection. hhh 6. Relations between Officers and Men.

Always very close in a unit of this sort, particularly when an officer may find himself alone with his patrol of ten men for weeks at a time. Any other arrangement would result in a decrease of efficiency. hhh 7. Relations Between other Services.

As we are always carried to the scene of our operations by R.N. or R.A.F., it is essential that our relations with these services are of a high order, as indeed they are. hhh 8 . Relations with Allies.

Relationship with Allied Troops has always been sound with the exception of the Jugo-slavs. Their persistently unhelpful and obstructionist attitude when this unit was endeavoring to assist them in the common cause, culminating in the "imprisonment" of one Sqn can hardly be said to have improved relations with this extraordinary nation. hhh 9. Home Affairs, Post-war and Release.
The men show great interest in these matters and seem well content with arrangements for release and plans made for their absorption into civil life again. hhh 10. Finance.
On the whole the men are satisfied with their pay. The number of irregularities by Paymaster concerning Parachute Pay seems to be decreasing. hhh 11. Miscellaneous.

(a) Overseas Service. - Most personnel have done well over three years' service overseas and are naturally pleased with the prospect of returning to U.K. in less than four-and-a-half years. Some forty all ranks are due for PYTHON within the next two or three months.
(b) Leave. - This unit does well as far as this is concerned and seems pleased with the leave facilities in C.M.F., particularly in Rome.
(c) Relations with civil population. - Dealings with the civil population are avoided where possible.
JTL/JSFM.
Lt.Col;
Commanding, Special Boat Service, C.M.F.
